---
name: Feature / task
about: New feature, refactor, bug fix, or chore. Fill every section — leave none blank.
title: "type(scope): short imperative description"
labels: ""
assignees: ""
---

## User story

As a [role], I want [capability], so that [benefit].

<!-- Add a second user story if there is a distinct second beneficiary. -->

## Background

<!--
Why does this issue exist? What problem or gap does it fix?
Include enough context for a coding agent with no prior knowledge of the codebase to understand the motivation.
Reference related issues, PRs, or docs where useful.
-->

## Acceptance criteria

### Happy path

**Given** [precondition]
**When** [action]
**Then** [expected result]

<!-- Add more Given/When/Then blocks for each distinct success scenario. -->

### Error path / edge case

**Given** [precondition]
**When** [action]
**Then** [expected result]

<!-- Add one block per meaningful error or edge case. -->

## Out of scope

<!--
Explicitly list what this issue does NOT cover.
This prevents scope creep and clarifies what a follow-up issue should handle.
-->

-

## Technical context

<!--
File paths, relevant functions, API endpoints, environment variables, data schemas, or library choices
an implementor needs to know. Be specific — include line numbers or code snippets where helpful.
-->

## Implementation details

<!--
Step-by-step description of what to build. Numbered list.
Each step should map to a single coherent code change.
Do not prescribe exact implementation if the approach should be left to the implementor,
but do specify constraints (e.g. "must be a server-side operation", "must be idempotent").
-->

1.

## Additional test scenarios

<!--
Test cases beyond the acceptance criteria above.
These become unit / integration / E2E test stubs for the implementor.
-->

-

## Hard constraints

<!--
Non-negotiable requirements: security properties, performance ceilings, API compatibility,
env var naming conventions, etc. These must be satisfied for the PR to be merged.
-->

-

## Dependency issues

<!--
List issues that MUST be merged before this one, and issues that CAN be merged in parallel.
Format: "Depends on #N (reason)" or "Independent of #N".
-->

-
